So I stayed in the hospital for I don't know how long, then I had to have, you know, you forget how to walk when you've been in the hospital, you've been laying down so long. And so I had to go through that, all that stuff. And then we had to stay there for six months because the biggest thing they have is rejection. That's the biggest thing, some of you I'm sure are some kind of transplants in here. And you have to take transplant medicine. There were five of us, four of us, five of us in my graduating class, myself and four other guys. And they're all gone, I'm the only one left. My wife's theory of why I'm still left is number one, you're listening to it, that everything is positive to me and I've had a magic life and everything. I've got wonderful kids and wonderful wife and it's sickening to listen to me. Because I just, I think there's been a magic spot made for me in this world and I just kind of went through it. And I don't know why and I don't know how, but it worked. And so that's my story, I don't know, let's do it anyway. What was the last question? Did I answer any, do you got any questions on my heart? Tell us about the mother. Oh, there, so the mother? The person that gave it? Yeah. That's a great story. You can get the name of the person that gave you the organ if the person that gave you the organ will allow it. So you call up the organ place and say, I'd like to get the person that got the organ, gave me the organ and would you contact him and have him contact me? So they did and she called me. She's a very nice lady from Alabama, little tiny town in Alabama. And she said I'm so-and-so and I was the boy's mother. This boy was a 22 year old boy that had gotten into drugs and was a drug dealer and got shot in a drug deal. That's what I think. She says he died. There was a drug dealer shot in Lexington the day before I got my heart. And that's what I think happened. I think that's who that was, but I never really found out exactly, okay? It might not have been. Anyway, I called her up and I said, I'm on my paddle boat in Hope, Alabama. We're going to Montagrand next couple of days. I want you to come and be with my family and go to the Montagrand with me. She said I'll be glad to. So she took a bus up to there and I heard the heart in my son. I heard the heart in my son. May I hear it in you? So I went over to her and pulled up my shirt and she listened to my heart and the whole table cried, including me. It was probably the most beautiful moment I've ever been in my life. It was just one of those moments that, you know, got a halo all over the top of it. But that's my story. Did I tell it right? Anything I've- Go. Thank you for sharing and for being here. Thank you. One of the questions that comes to mind is that I've run several successful businesses and I've been very blessed with a lot of things as well. One of the things I'm always looking for is just some degree, the next big opportunity that we're trying to attach to our business or so that we can help other people. And you went through a process where you were getting ready to get shut down so you went and found another business. If it were, you had one successful business and you kept adding on to it. If you had to do it now, what would you go look for? What would be the aspects? What would you look for now to find that next successful thing? I mean, technology really is what you latched onto then. And I did the same thing and I latched onto it some day. Well, you've got to understand what I really did. I can't run a computer. I get that. I understand that. I don't run it, but it's beyond me. I couldn't do typing. I graduated from Army Type School. You had to type 18 words a minute to graduate. I took less for six weeks, eight hours a day. And I was supposed to type 18 words and I cheated on two of those to get out of there. So I don't do anything with my fingers. My mouth's the only thing that works on me. Your question is, what would I do now? You bet. To find the next big thing. Well, I think what I did then was so powerful that I would find five businesses that were working right now and copy them and star all over again. I think my concept of saying to them that if one doesn't work, you can have another one is so powerful, I definitely would use that. It's comfortable. So what I heard you say was you'd go find five successful businesses and basically try to regrow or duplicate one of those. I would duplicate them, change them. And what I then have is if you seek call me, I say to you that you have a business, but with me you get CDE and F business too. And the other guy only has a business. Now if you're gonna go with them, who are you gonna go with? All right, thank you very much. You're welcome.
